title: Patcheeswarar temple
tagline: Patcheeswarar temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Patcheeswarar temple is a temple located in Cheyyar, Tiruvannamalai District, Tamil Nadu, India built during the times of Pallava dynasty.
overview.description: Patcheeswarar temple is a temple located in Cheyyar, Tiruvannamalai District, Tamil Nadu, India built during the times of Pallava dynasty. It is a temple of Lord Shiva, built in Dravidian architecture.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Cheyyar taluk
topPlaces.description: Cheyyaru taluk is a taluk of Thiruvannamalai district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters is the town of Cheyyar.

topPlaces.name: Cheyyar
topPlaces.description: Cheyyar, officially Tiruvetipuram is a Town in the Tiruvannamalai District in the Tamil Nadu state of South India. The most common name of this town is Cheyyar but it is also referred to as Tiruvetipuram in many government records.

topPlaces.name: Kazhiyur
topPlaces.description: Kazhiyur or Kaliyur is a village in the Cheyyur taluk of the Chengalpattu district in the Tamil Nadu state of India. It is located on the way from Kancheepuram to Cheyyar.

topPlaces.name: Madipakkam, Tiruvannamalai
topPlaces.description: Madipakkam is a village located in the Tiruvannamalai district of Tamil Nadu, in southern India. According to local tradition and historical references, the village was originally known as Madumaanagar.

topPlaces.name: Kovilur, Tiruvannamalai
topPlaces.description: Kovilur is a village in the Tiruvannamalai District near Cheyyar Town in the Tamil Nadu state of South India and have a population of 5,123.

topPlaces.name: Agastheesvarar Temple, Purisai
topPlaces.description: Agastheesvarar Temple, Purisai is a Siva temple in Purisai in Tiruvannamalai district in Tamil Nadu (India).

topPlaces.name: Purisai
topPlaces.description: Purisai is a village panchayat of Cheyyar Taluk. The village is famous for temples and festivals.

items.note: Cheyyaru taluk is a taluk of Thiruvannamalai district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters is the town of Cheyyar.

Cheyyar, officially Tiruvetipuram is a Town in the Tiruvannamalai District in the Tamil Nadu state of South India. The most common name of this town is Cheyyar but it is also referred to as Tiruvetipuram in many government records.
Kazhiyur or Kaliyur is a village in the Cheyyur taluk of the Chengalpattu district in the Tamil Nadu state of India. It is located on the way from Kancheepuram to Cheyyar.

Madipakkam is a village located in the Tiruvannamalai district of Tamil Nadu, in southern India. According to local tradition and historical references, the village was originally known as Madumaanagar.
Kovilur is a village in the Tiruvannamalai District near Cheyyar Town in the Tamil Nadu state of South India and have a population of 5,123.
Agastheesvarar Temple, Purisai is a Siva temple in Purisai in Tiruvannamalai district in Tamil Nadu (India).
Purisai is a village panchayat of Cheyyar Taluk. The village is famous for temples and festivals.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Patcheeswarar temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Patcheeswarar temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Patcheeswarar temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (78 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (92 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Patcheeswarar temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Patcheeswarar temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Patcheeswarar temple?
faq.a: Stays in Patcheeswarar temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Patcheeswarar temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Cheyyar taluk, Cheyyar, Kazhiyur, Madipakkam, Tiruvannamalai, Kovilur, Tiruvannamalai.
